_comment: Parity-harness whitelist (issue #78 Phase 2c). Entries here are KNOWN-BETTER
deviations of semgraph from CodeGraph 0.9.7, recorded in ADR-003/ADR-004.
A whitelisted diff is reported separately and does NOT count as a parity
failure. Every entry carries a justification. Do not add an entry to paper
over a genuine regression — only for a documented, intentional improvement.
qualified_name/file/source/target accept a simple glob where '*' matches any run.

node_attrs.justification: ADR-003: semgraph sets is_async correctly for EVERY language (Rust async fn, Python async def), whereas CodeGraph 0.9.7 flags only TypeScript. Where our is_async differs it is authoritative and better; is_async is not part of the node match key, so this never affects the node match percentage.

ADR-003: semgraph captures clean, complete docstrings (Rust /// doc comments, TS/JS block comments) where CodeGraph 0.9.7 leaves Python NULL, keeps a stray leading '/'/'!' on Rust doc comments, bleeds a module //! header into the first definition, and misses export-wrapped TS declarations.

edges.justification: ADR-004: CodeGraph 0.9.7 double-emits the return-type reference for TS signatures whose return type is nested in a generic (Array<...>/Promise<...>). semgraph emits each type occurrence exactly once. 'only_duplicates' whitelists a missing instance ONLY when semgraph still emits that (source,target,references) key at least once, i.e. a duplicate-multiplicity omission — a true recall gap (zero of our side) is never whitelisted.
